name: GPT-5.2 Pro
tagline: OpenAI's extended-thinking Pro tier for research-grade problems.
metaDescription: GPT-5.2 Pro is OpenAI's highest-effort reasoning tier, released Dec 11, 2025. 400K context, vision input, top verified ARC-AGI-2 score. See specs, benchmarks a…
overview: GPT-5.2 Pro is the top reasoning tier of OpenAI's GPT-5.2 family, released on December 11, 2025. Inside ChatGPT, GPT-5.2 ships in three modes — Instant, Thinking, and Pro — where Pro spends the most test-time reasoning compute. OpenAI positions GPT-5.2 Pro as its smartest and most trustworthy model for difficult questions where a higher-quality answer is worth the wait, showing fewer major errors than GPT-5.2 Thinking across complex domains like programming and science.

Technically, GPT-5.2 Pro shares the GPT-5.2 family envelope: a 400,000-token context window, up to 128,000 output tokens, an August 31, 2025 knowledge cutoff, and text plus image (vision) input. It is exposed in the OpenAI API through the Responses API as the model id gpt-5.2-pro. The launch followed an internal 'Code Red' push, arriving roughly three weeks after Google's Gemini 3 Pro.

GPT-5.2 Pro briefly held the verified state-of-the-art on ARC-AGI-2: the ARC Prize team independently confirmed 54.2% for the Pro (High) setting, ahead of GPT-5.2 Thinking's 52.9%. OpenAI has since released GPT-5.4 and GPT-5.5, so GPT-5.2 Pro is now documented as a previous-generation model, but it remains a notable data point for how far extra reasoning compute pushed frontier accuracy in late 2025.
strengths: Top verified ARC-AGI-2 result at launch — ARC Prize independently confirmed 54.2% for GPT-5.2 Pro (High), the best on that benchmark at the time
Highest-effort reasoning tier: spends more test-time compute than GPT-5.2 Thinking for fewer major errors on hard problems
Strong graduate-level science and competition math — 93.2% GPQA Diamond and 100% on AIME 2025 (vendor-reported)
Large 400K-token context with 128K max output for long, multi-step professional work
Vision input — reads charts, screenshots and interface layouts, not just text
useCases: Research-grade questions where a slower, higher-quality answer is worth the wait
Graduate-level science and advanced math reasoning
Complex, multi-step programming and debugging tasks
Long-context analysis across large documents (up to ~400K tokens)
Chart, screenshot and interface understanding via vision input
Knowledge-work deliverables such as spreadsheets, financial models and presentations

links.label: ARC Prize — GPT-5.2 Pro verified ARC-AGI-2 result
GPT-5.2 vs Gemini 3.0 vs Claude Opus 4.5 — R&D World
Sam Altman on GPT-5.2 launch — CNBC

status: Superseded by GPT-5.4 / GPT-5.5; still documented in OpenAI's API as a previous-generation model.
architecture: Proprietary GPT-5.2-family reasoning model. Pro is the highest-effort variant of the line: it spends more test-time reasoning compute than GPT-5.2 Thinking, trading latency for accuracy on hard problems. OpenAI has not published parameter counts or architecture internals.

pricing.note: Token prices shown are for the GPT-5.2 family API (gpt-5.2). ARC Prize separately verified a measured cost of $15.72 per task for GPT-5.2 Pro (High) on ARC-AGI-2. OpenAI's current pricing page no longer lists GPT-5.2 after the GPT-5.4 / GPT-5.5 releases; third-party Pro per-token figures disagree, so they are omitted.

faq.a: OpenAI released the GPT-5.2 family — Instant, Thinking, and Pro — on December 11, 2025, beginning with paid ChatGPT plans. GPT-5.2 Pro is the highest-effort reasoning tier of that family.

faq.q: How is GPT-5.2 Pro different from GPT-5.2 Thinking?
faq.a: Both are reasoning models, but Pro spends more test-time reasoning compute than Thinking. OpenAI positions it as the smartest, most trustworthy option for difficult questions where a higher-quality answer is worth waiting longer for, with fewer major errors on complex tasks.

faq.q: What is GPT-5.2 Pro's context window and knowledge cutoff?
faq.a: GPT-5.2 Pro shares the family envelope: a 400,000-token context window, up to 128,000 output tokens, and an August 31, 2025 knowledge cutoff. It accepts text and image (vision) input, per OpenAI's API documentation.

faq.q: How good is GPT-5.2 Pro on ARC-AGI-2?
faq.a: The ARC Prize team independently verified GPT-5.2 Pro (High) at 54.2% on ARC-AGI-2 — state-of-the-art at the time — ahead of GPT-5.2 Thinking's 52.9%, at a measured cost of about $15.72 per task.
